articles.title: VIBE CODING CAN'T MAKE A WEBSITE BEAUTIFUL
articles.summary: AI can spin up functional landing pages - but distinctive, impactful websites are still human made. And with Framer [4], designers can lead the way in creating beautiful websites, with no developers required.

articles.title: THE FUTURE OF DESIGN IS CODE AND CANVAS
articles.summary: Figma now lets users send work from Claude Code directly into fully editable Figma layers by installing the Figma MCP and typing a simple command. The company argues that the design canvas is better suited than code editors for exploring multiple possibilities, comparing approaches, and refining details. This new workflow reflects a shift from linear design-to-code processes toward starting anywhere and using Figma as the central hub.

articles.title: WORDPRESS' NEW AI ASSISTANT WILL LET USERS EDIT THEIR SITES WITH PROMPTS
articles.summary: WordPress launched an opt-in AI assistant integrated into its site editor and media library capable of generating images, editing and translating text, and reorganizing sites. Users can interact with it via a sidebar or by tagging "@ai" in block notes, a feature added in the 6.9 update. The tool must be manually enabled in settings, except for sites built with WordPress's AI website builder.

articles.title: PIXEL 10A HANDS-ON: FLAT CAMERA BAR DESIGN WOWS
articles.summary: The Pixel 10a refines its minimalist design with a fully flat camera bar, improved durability (Gorilla Glass 7i, recycled aluminum frame, and IP68), and a brighter 6.3-inch OLED display that peaks at 3,000 nits, while keeping the Tensor G4 chip and familiar 48MP camera system. With Android 16, seven years of updates, practical upgrades like satellite SOS, Wi-Fi 6E, a stable modem, and a 5,100mAh battery with faster charging, it focuses on everyday usability and value over headline specs, starting at $499.

articles.title: CONTEXT IS YOUR CREATIVE LEVERAGE
articles.summary: Providing AI with the right amount of context — not too little, not too much — is key to getting useful outputs. The skill lies in curating a personal library of high-signal material and strategically selecting only what's relevant for each task. Well-curated context becomes reusable creative capital that compounds over time, turning AI from a wild guesser into a well-informed collaborator.

articles.title: TYPOGRAPHY MIGHT BE THE LAST THING AI CAN'T FAKE
articles.summary: Typography is the backbone of branding, yet most widely available fonts are either blandly interchangeable or too expressive to function across full brand systems, leaving few options that balance personality with practicality. As branding has prioritized efficiency and scalability, much of the emotional distinctiveness that once defined iconic brands has faded, and while custom type offers strong ownability, it's often too costly for many teams. In an AI-driven landscape where visuals are increasingly homogenized, thoughtfully crafted, human-made typography may become one of the clearest signals of authenticity and distinction, helping brands stand out by proving real intention and care.

articles.title: THE HIDDEN COST OF AI PROTOTYPES THAT ARE MADE TO DIE
articles.summary: AI app-building tools have made creating polished, functional prototypes fast and routine, but many are designed as disposable artifacts that can't easily evolve into production, leading to costly rebuilds and translation work later. Different tools optimize for different “lifespans”—from quick ideation (design-native generators) to platform-bound publishing (visual builders) to more durable, production-ready outputs (full-stack generators)—so teams must choose based on whether a prototype is meant to validate an idea or become a foundation for real product development. Ultimately, the challenge is no longer speed, but continuity: ensuring AI-generated work can extend, integrate, and mature rather than being thrown away after validation.

articles.title: E-COMMERCE DESIGN INSPIRATION
articles.summary: Browse the best-designed e-commerce websites in one place: filter page type, industry, style, and view on desktop or mobile.

articles.title: AI PRESENTATION MAKER
articles.summary: Dokie is your AI presentation agent—generate professional slides instantly, customize design styles, refine content effortlessly, and maintain clean layouts with every edit.

articles.title: BUILD CHATGPT APPS WITHOUT CODE
articles.summary: Chippy is where PMs and designers prototype ChatGPT apps, engineers export production specs, and teams measure what's working.

articles.title: COMMON DESIGN RED FLAGS FROM YOUR WEBSITE YOU MIGHT BE MISSING
articles.summary: Websites often fail not because of poor aesthetics but due to subtle experience problems that quietly erode engagement and trust. Research shows users judge credibility within milliseconds and abandon slow-loading pages rapidly, making mobile optimization, clear visual hierarchy, and concise value propositions essential for conversion. Addressing these overlooked design red flags through user behavior data, testing, and iterative improvements can unlock meaningful, sustainable growth.

articles.title: KEEP IT MOVIN': FLUORO®'S DYNAMIC VISUAL IDENTITY FOR SCOTTISH HARD DANCE LABEL DOOF
articles.summary: Hannah Laing's label and event series Doof has teamed up with Fluoro on a dynamic new identity that captures the energy of hard dance while evolving beyond its underground roots. The rebrand introduces a more kinetic, onomatopoeic logotype and a movement-led visual system of pulsing graphics, gritty textures, and after-dark tones, spanning stage visuals, festival posters, and apparel. Rejecting minimal “techno-goth” trends, Doof positions itself as bold, inclusive, and community-driven as it grows within the UK dance scene.

articles.title: HOW WE REFRESHED THE GERMAN NATIONAL FOOTBALL LEAGUE IDENTITY... 5 TIMES
articles.summary: Since appointing Mutabor in 2010, the DFL has evolved the Bundesliga into a global sports brand through five major refreshes, transforming a glossy 3D logo into a dynamic, motion-first design system rooted in football's energy and culture. Key milestones include the introduction of audio branding, a custom typeface, digital design tools, motion-led systems, and most recently a more tactile, 3D-infused identity that adds emotional depth while keeping the iconic player symbol at its core. Through a long-term hybrid partnership between its in-house team and Mutabor, the Bundesliga has built a flexible, globally scalable brand that reflects both shifting design trends and the league's authentic fan-driven identity.

articles.title: WHY THE AI CARICATURE TREND MAKES SO MANY CREATIVES UNCOMFORTABLE
articles.summary: The viral AI caricature trend raises serious concerns for creatives because the tools rely on vast visual data, often sourced from real artists without their consent.

articles.title: HOW DESIGNERS CAN AVOID BURNOUT FROM ENDLESS CLIENT REVISIONS
articles.summary: Endless client revision loops can be prevented by adopting a "Rule of Three" for contract revisions and requiring consolidated feedback lists.
